author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
śuna he madhumathana! mama eka vijñāpana,
viśeṣa kariyā bali āmi
tomāra śeṣatva mama, svakīya vaibhavottama,
āmi dāsa, tumi mora svāmī

se⋅vibhava⋅bahirbhūta, haite haile, he acyuta,
kṣaṇamātra sahite nā pāri
deha⋅prāṇa⋅sukha āśā, ātmaprati bhālabāsā,
sarvatyāga karite vicāri

e saba jāuka nāśa, śatabāra śrīnivāsa,
tabu thāku dāsatva tomāra
e bhaktivinoda kaya, kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa jīva haya,
dāsya vinā kibā āche āra
en_translation:
O Hari! Listen, O Madhumathana! I have one submission, which I especially say: I am subordinate to You, a part of Your own supreme opulence. I am Your servant, and You are my master.

O Acyuta, if I were to be excluded from that opulence, I could not tolerate it for even a moment. I resolve to give up everything: the hope for bodily and vital pleasures, and love for myself.

Let all these perish a hundred times, O Śrīnivāsa, but let my servitude to You remain. Bhaktivinoda says: the soul is a servant of Kṛṣṇa; what else is there without servitude?
